mentions.note: New CEO taking over from James Quincy; James's last quarter; no snack business help like Pepsi.
CPG industry weak except Coca-Cola, which churns higher. New CEO Enrique Braun takes helm; predecessor James Quincey steps to chairman.
Example of stock many investors love and refuse to sell despite changed fundamentals; Cramer warns against falling in love with any stock regardless of sentiment.
Bessembinder research identified Coca-Cola as one of 17 stocks with 5 million percent returns; consistent dividend but low single-digit growth; consumer staple.
Rallied 3% despite market chaos; quintessential defensive stock under new CEO RK Brun continuing growth strategy. Continue to buy on S&P weakness.

Used as a PE valuation example — Coke's ~$55 share price is irrelevant; what matters is it trades at ~19× earnings; cited to illustrate that nominal share price carries no valuation meaning without the PE multiple context.
